data.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization). Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes for THIS child (S4, merge G3+G4) against the whole sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. ONE G4 FINDING (scope-overlap / conflicting-assumption: the required per-child output guardrail is owned by S5 but load-bearing for S4 and absent from S4's spec). Affirmations and the finding below.

AFFIRMATIONS (modes that read CLEAN under ANTI-FP):

- Implicit shared state: `sizing.py` container-cost projection (`len(cont)*n_children*COST_AGENT`, sizing.py:206) is touched by THREE siblings in sequence — S3 (cost-model fix + "container never shed"), S4 (this child: 2N->N), S5 (<N after packing). Real shared state, but EXPLICITLY ordering-resolved by the parent's Sequencing note ("Land S3 -> S4 -> S5 STRICTLY in DAG order: S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's 'container never shed' + cost-model fix lands") and the DAG (S3->S4->S5). S4's own remediation AC ("sizing.py container-cost projection reflects N calls; shedding stays calibrated") correctly layers on S3's fix. No contradiction.

- Dependency gap: NONE. The DAG S3->S4 and S6->S4 are both modeled (S3 ba7e `blocks` 98c6; S6 da34 `blocks` 98c6), and S4 `blocks` S5 (1762) — matching the parent DAG `S1->{S2,S3}; S3->S4; S6->{S4,S5}; S4->S5`. S4's remediation correctly re-points the fidelity gate from "vague offline eval" to the S6 semantic harness (DEPENDS ON S6). Incoming deps consistent.

- Conflicting assumptions (cost/shedding): S4's "halve container calls" premise is consistent with S3's de-risk-D finding (halving container cost pushes the shed threshold past N=40 but S3's stronger "never shed" rule is the robust fix) — S4 is positioned as a major MITIGATION, not a competing fix. No conflict.

- Ordering violation: NONE intrinsic — S4 is the middle node of the strict S3->S4->S5 chain; it takes the sizing projection from 2N to N, S5 then takes it N->{<N}. Correctly sequenced.

- Consumer impact: S4 restructures the per-call criterion vocabulary (1 criterion -> 2) over the call structure S3 produces (new pool + warm-then-fan-out); S5 consumes S4's merged-call shape. Producer->consumer is DAG-ordered. The attribution remediation (validate `criteria` against {G3,G4}, drop out-of-set, preserve `_container_child`, mirror passes.py:222-224) correctly protects the coach/sidecar downstream consumers of the finding->criterion mapping. Consistent.

- Residual references: S4's body still says "Gate behind an OFFLINE EVAL"/"offline eval shows no accuracy regression" while the remediation supersedes this with "Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness (DEPENDS ON S6), not a vague 'offline eval'." This is a stale label within S4, superseded in-ticket by its own remediation — an intra-child editorial residue, not a cross-child contradiction (S6's own ticket carries the matching semantic-harness contract). Below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold; recorded for editorial cleanup only, NOT a G4 finding.

G4 FINDING:

- finding: The "REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail" is owned by sibling S5 (1762: "Structured per-child sections in the prompt + REQUIRED per-child output so per-child attention isn't lost") but is load-bearing for S4 and ABSENT from S4's spec — a scope/ownership split that leaves S4 without the guardrail it depends on. The shared meta-evidence comment (carried on S4/S5/S6, ts 1782572460/463/466) states the batching tail-child recency/salience bias "risks per-child DEPTH on tail children" and that the guardrail "(already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must ALSO apply to S4." S4 already batches G3+G4 over a (child/bin) and is the FIRST batching child in the DAG (S4->S5), so the very fidelity hazard the guardrail mitigates is present at S4, yet S4's What/AC mention no per-child structured-output requirement. Without it, S4's own AC ("findings correctly attributed to G3 vs G4"; "no accuracy regression within tolerance") can pass on aggregate counts while silently under-reviewing a tail child — a cross-child consistency gap between S4 and S5 (and an unmet obligation flagged by the shared S6 note).
  criteria: [G4]
  location: child 98c6 ("## What" / "## Acceptance Criteria"); cross-ref sibling 1762 S5 "## What" (REQUIRED per-child output) + shared comment ts 1782572460471843001
  evidence:
    - "S5 (1762) body: 'Structured per-child sections in the prompt + REQUIRED per-child output so per-child attention isn't lost' — the guardrail lives in S5 only."
    - "Shared meta-comment on S4/S5/S6: 'the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must also apply to S4. S6's eval should specifically check TAIL-child recall.'"
    - "S4 '## What' specifies the merged dual-rubric call + attribution but no per-child/bin structured-output completeness requirement; S4 'Why' already frames the call as 'per (child/bin)', i.e. it can batch >1 child, so the tail-recall hazard applies at S4."
    - "Mode = scope overlap + conflicting assumption: the guardrail's ownership sits in S5 while the dependency on it arises one step earlier in S4 (S4->S5), so under strict DAG ordering S4 lands WITHOUT it."
  scenarios:
    - "S4 lands (S5 not yet started). A merged G3+G4 call over a 2-child bin attends mostly to the head child; the tail child gets a shallow pass. Aggregate G3/G4 finding counts match the separate-call baseline within tolerance, so S4's AC and the S6 recall metric (if it does not check per-child/tail recall) both pass — but the tail child's child-coverage/consistency was effectively unreviewed. Fidelity AC silently violated."
  impact: "S4 can satisfy its tolerance/attribution AC while regressing per-child (tail) review depth, because the per-child completeness guardrail that prevents this is scoped to the LATER sibling S5. The parent's fidelity AC ('no fidelity regression vs current per-child behavior') is at risk for the first batching child."
  checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes — scope overlap / conflicting assumptions (the per-child guardrail S4 depends on is owned by a later sibling and absent from S4)."
  suggested_fix: "Add to S4's '## What' and AC a REQUIRED structured per-child/per-bin output guarantee (each child id in the bin returns a finding list OR an explicit 'clean' marker), mirroring S5's guardrail, so the merged call cannot silently omit a tail child; and have the S6 fidelity gate (S4's declared gate) explicitly check TAIL-child recall, per the shared meta-comment. Either pull the guardrail forward into S4 or make S4's fidelity AC depend on S5's guardrail landing first."

Note (NOT a G4 finding, recorded for editorial cleanup): S4's body 'OFFLINE EVAL' language is superseded by its own remediation ('Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness') — an intra-child stale label, consistent across the roster (S6 carries the matching semantic-harness contract). Reads benignly under ANTI-FP.
